title: Traffic-related air pollution and respiratory symptoms among asthmatic children, resident in Mexico City: the EVA cohort study.
ehr_evidence: The study was a prospective longitudinal panel study where children were invited to participate and followed up for an average of 22 weeks with daily records of symptoms and medication.
summary: A prospective longitudinal study (EVA cohort) evaluating the impact of traffic-related air pollution (PM2.5, NO2, O3, and diesel-fueled vehicles) on respiratory symptoms and medication use in asthmatic and healthy children in Mexico City.
key_findings: Wheezing was significantly associated with PM2.5, NO2, and O3.
Diesel-fueled motor vehicles were significantly associated with wheezing and bronchodilator use.
population: 147 asthmatic and 50 healthy children in Mexico City
